extraction.one_line_summary: FREIER Laurens attacked inside final kilometer after infiltrating late Costa-Ron-Keukeleire breakaway, held off faster sprinters to win.
tactical_patterns: Breakaway rider traded mountain points for company, letting Freier take KOM classification points to maintain cooperative pace dynamic.
Ron attacked multiple times but refused to commit alone, revealing defensive tactics when riding for stage wins versus GC.
Costa refused to pull through with Ron despite both hunting stage win, sacrificing breakaway cohesion for sprint advantage.
Freier pre-committed to long sprint from 400m knowing inferior speed required gap-building versus Costa/Keukeleire/Visconti finishers.
Dimension Data work rate in breakaway suggests team prioritizes stage hunting over protecting 2:23 leader Dumoulin's margin.

rider_signals.FREIER Laurens: positive: recovered from early climb suffering to win from breakaway, showing multi-terrain resilience rarely expected from Dimension Data domestiques
rider_signals.PINO Wout: neutral: attacked with 75km remaining but immediately looked for others to share workload, not riding with stage-winning conviction
rider_signals.RON Pierre: negative: repeatedly attacked but refused solo commitment despite having time trial form from previous day, tactically indecisive
rider_signals.COSTA Rui: negative: refused cooperation with Ron in break despite shared interest, prioritizing sprint over enlarging advantage
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: punctured mid-stage, confirmed mechanical vulnerability pattern continuing from earlier Giro stages
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: neutral: sitting 2:23 ahead overall but team still committing riders to breaks rather than pure GC defense
